description: Frequency ID game — ear training game where the user identifies the frequency of an EQ boost or cut applied to pink noise. Monochrome DAW aesthetic, 5 rounds per game, desktop hover+click and mobile landscape touch UX.

userStories.title: Audio engine — pink noise with parametric EQ
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a browser audio engine that plays pink noise with a peaking EQ filter at a given frequency so the game can present audio challenges.
acceptanceCriteria: File src/lib/audio.ts exports class AudioEngine
AudioEngine has method init(): void — creates an AudioContext (lazy, called once on first user gesture)

AudioEngine has method play(freq: number, gainDb: number): void — plays pink noise with a BiquadFilterNode (type 'peaking', frequency = freq, gain = gainDb, Q = 2.5) for 3 seconds then stops; gainDb is +12 for boost, -12 for cut

Pink noise approximation: use a white-noise source (AudioBufferSourceNode with a buffer filled with Math.random() * 2 - 1) passed through three cascaded IIRFilterNode or BiquadFilterNode with type 'lowshelf'/'highshelf' to approximate a pink noise spectrum; alternatively, use a single AudioBufferSourceNode with pre-generated pink noise buffer (1 second, looping)
AudioEngine has method stop(): void — disconnects current source and filter, ignores if nothing playing
AudioEngine has method isPlaying: boolean getter — returns whether audio is currently playing
SSR guard: all AudioContext usage gated on typeof window !== 'undefined'
Export a singleton: export const audioEngine = new AudioEngine()
pnpm check passes
pnpm lint passes

userStories.title: Frequency strip component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see and interact with a horizontal frequency strip (20 Hz – 20 kHz, log scale) so I can point to the frequency I think the EQ is applied at.
acceptanceCriteria: Component at src/lib/components/freq-strip.svelte
Props (Svelte 5 $props()): onSelect: (freq: number) => void, disabled?: boolean (default false)
Internal state ($state): hoveredFreq: number | null = null

Strip is a full-width <div> with role='slider' aria-label='Frequency selector' aria-valuemin=20 aria-valuemax=20000; height 80px on desktop, 120px on mobile (use min-h-[80px] md:min-h-[80px] sm:min-h-[120px] or similar)
Background: bg-card border border-border rounded; cursor crosshair when not disabled
Log-scale mapping: posToFreq(x, width) = 20 * Math.pow(20000/20, x/width); freqToPos(freq, width) = width * Math.log(freq/20) / Math.log(20000/20)
On mousemove (desktop): compute hoveredFreq from event.offsetX and element clientWidth; update $state
On mouseleave (desktop): set hoveredFreq = null
On click (desktop): if not disabled call onSelect(hoveredFreq)
On touchstart (mobile): prevent default; compute freq from touch.clientX relative to getBoundingClientRect; set hoveredFreq
On touchmove (mobile): prevent default; update hoveredFreq from touch.clientX
On touchend (mobile): if not disabled call onSelect(hoveredFreq); set hoveredFreq = null
Visual: when hoveredFreq is not null, render a vertical line (absolute, 1px wide, bg-foreground/70, full height) at freqToPos(hoveredFreq, width)

Frequency label: when hoveredFreq is not null, render the frequency formatted as '120 Hz' or '1.2 kHz' (values ≥1000 shown as kHz with 1 decimal) in a small badge (text-xs, bg-background border border-border px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded, absolute, above the cursor line, clamped to strip bounds)

Frequency tick marks: render ticks at [20, 50, 100, 200, 500, 1000, 2000, 5000, 10000, 20000] Hz — short vertical lines (2px wide, 8px tall, bg-border) at the bottom of the strip with labels below (text-[10px] text-muted-foreground); labels: '20', '50', '100', '200', '500', '1k', '2k', '5k', '10k', '20k'
When disabled: opacity-40 cursor-not-allowed, no hover/touch response
Use the Svelte MCP (list-sections then get-documentation) before writing the .svelte file
Run svelte-autofixer on the .svelte file and fix all reported issues
pnpm check passes
pnpm lint passes

userStories.title: Game logic — rounds, scoring, error margin
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a typed game-state helper so the page can manage 5 rounds, evaluate guesses within an error margin, and track the session score.
acceptanceCriteria: File src/lib/game-frequency-id.ts exports types and functions
Export type RoundResult = 'correct' | 'wrong' | 'pending'
Export type Round = { targetFreq: number; gainDb: number; guess: number | null; result: RoundResult }
Export type GameState = { rounds: Round[]; currentRound: number; phase: 'idle' | 'playing' | 'guessing' | 'roundResult' | 'gameOver' }

Export function newGame(): GameState — creates 5 rounds with random targetFreq (log-uniform: 20 * Math.pow(20000/20, Math.random()), rounded to nearest integer) and random gainDb (+12 or -12, 50/50 chance), phase 'idle', currentRound 0
Export function startRound(state: GameState): GameState — returns new state with phase 'playing' (immutable, return new object)
Export function markPlaying(state: GameState): GameState — returns new state with phase 'guessing' (audio has finished, user can now guess)

Export function submitGuess(state: GameState, guess: number): GameState — evaluates guess against targetFreq using ±1/3 octave margin (correct if Math.abs(Math.log2(guess/targetFreq)) <= 1/3), sets round result, advances phase to 'roundResult'
Export function nextRound(state: GameState): GameState — advances currentRound, sets phase to 'idle' if more rounds remain, or 'gameOver' if all 5 rounds done
Export function scoreCorrect(state: GameState): number — returns count of rounds with result 'correct'
Export const ERROR_MARGIN_OCTAVES = 1/3
No side effects; all functions are pure (return new GameState objects)
pnpm check passes
pnpm lint passes

userStories.title: Frequency ID game page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to play the Frequency ID game at /games/frequency-id so I can train my ear to identify EQ frequencies.
acceptanceCriteria: Route at src/routes/games/frequency-id/+page.svelte
<svelte:head> sets <title>Frequency ID — Auris</title>
Page uses audioEngine singleton from src/lib/audio.ts and game functions from src/lib/game-frequency-id.ts
Page layout: max-w-2xl mx-auto px-4 py-8, dark background inherited from layout

IDLE phase UI (before first round starts): show round indicator (e.g. 'ROUND 1 / 5'), a large PLAY button (shadcn-svelte Button, size='lg'), the FreqStrip with disabled=true, and a hint text 'Press PLAY to hear the audio'

PLAYING phase UI: PLAY button shows 'PLAYING...' and is disabled; FreqStrip is disabled; audio is playing (call audioEngine.play(targetFreq, gainDb) on phase entry); after 3.1 seconds, auto-transition to GUESSING phase via setTimeout

GUESSING phase UI: instruction text 'Click the frequency you hear' (desktop) or 'Hold to aim, release to submit' (mobile, detected via pointer: coarse media query or $state flag); FreqStrip is enabled; no play button shown; user can click/touch FreqStrip to submit guess
ROUND_RESULT phase UI: show whether correct or wrong, show target frequency formatted ('1.2 kHz' / '120 Hz'), show user's guess frequency, show a NEXT ROUND button (or FINISH if last round)
GAME_OVER phase UI: show 'GAME OVER', show score as 'X / 5', show list of all 5 rounds with target freq, guess, and correct/wrong indicator; show PLAY AGAIN button that resets game to new state
Score display: always visible header showing current score (correct count) during the game
Round indicator: always shows 'ROUND N / 5' during active game
Boost/Cut indicator: in GUESSING and ROUND_RESULT phase, show whether the EQ was a 'BOOST' or 'CUT' (so user knows what to listen for; the frequency is unknown)
REPLAY button: in GUESSING phase, allow replaying the audio once more (call audioEngine.play again)
FreqStrip receives onSelect that calls submitGuess from game logic and updates $state game
Use the Svelte MCP (list-sections then get-documentation) before writing the .svelte file
Run svelte-autofixer on the .svelte file and fix all reported issues
pnpm check passes
pnpm lint passes
Verify in browser: http://localhost:5173/games/frequency-id — game renders, PLAY button triggers audio, freq strip is interactive after audio, submitting shows result, 5 rounds then game over

The other two cards (EQ Matching, Dynamics) remain available: false
